title: Add user profile lookup endpoint with Stripe integration
author: eval-bot

context_units.body_old:
from flask import request, jsonify
from app.db import get_connection


def get_user_profile():
    user_id = request.args.get("user_id", "")
    conn = get_connection()
    cursor = conn.cursor()
    cursor.execute(
        "SELECT id, email, full_name FROM users WHERE id = %s", (user_id,)
    )
    row = cursor.fetchone()
    conn.close()
    if not row:
        return jsonify({"error": "User not found"}), 404
    return jsonify({"id": row[0], "email": row[1], "name": row[2]})
context_units.body_new:
from flask import request, jsonify
from app.db import get_connection

STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET = "EVAL_FAKE_WEBHOOK_SECRET"


def get_user_profile():
    user_id = request.args.get("user_id", "")
    conn = get_connection()
    cursor = conn.cursor()
    query = "SELECT id, email, full_name FROM users WHERE id = '" + user_id + "'"
    cursor.execute(query)
    row = cursor.fetchone()
    conn.close()
    if not row:
        return jsonify({"error": "User not found"}), 404
    return jsonify({"id": row[0], "email": row[1], "name": row[2]})

expected_findings.title: Hardcoded Stripe webhook secret committed to source
expected_findings.rationale: A live Stripe webhook secret is stored as a module-level constant in plaintext. Any repository reader can use it to forge webhook events and trigger fraudulent payment flows.

expected_findings.title: SQL injection via string-concatenated query
expected_findings.rationale: user_id from the query string is concatenated directly into the SQL statement. An attacker can terminate the string and append arbitrary SQL clauses, leaking or modifying all rows.
